team_members.name: Nathan Allman
team_members.notes: Former Goldman Sachs digital assets team. Founded Ondo Finance in 2021. Publicly visible and frequently quoted in press. Holds economics degree from Dartmouth.

team_members.name: Pinku Surana
team_members.notes: Former Google engineer. Technical co-founder responsible for protocol architecture. Publicly visible on LinkedIn and in project communications.

team_members.name: Ian De Bode
team_members.notes: Was listed as co-founder at launch; no longer listed on Ondo's current team page as of 2024. Departure details NOT_AVAILABLE_FREE_TIER.

team_members.name: Justin Schmidt
prior_projects: Goldman Sachs (Head of Digital Asset Markets)
team_members.notes: Highly credentialed TradFi-to-crypto executive. Former Head of Digital Asset Markets at Goldman Sachs. Joined Ondo post-launch, significantly boosting institutional credibility.

team_members.name: Katie Wheeler
prior_projects: Traditional finance compliance roles (specific firms NOT_AVAILABLE_FREE_TIER)
team_members.notes: Listed on Ondo's website as CCO. Specific LinkedIn profile not confirmed via free search. Presence of a dedicated CCO signals compliance-first posture consistent with RWA tokenization focus.

ONDO token launched January 2024 on major exchanges. Public float is a minority of total supply at launch per tokenomics docs.

legal_events.description: Ondo Finance restructured its product offerings, discontinuing its original Ondo Vaults (structured DeFi yield products) and pivoting fully to tokenized US Treasuries (OUSG) to reduce regulatory exposure. This was a proactive compliance decision, not an enforcement action.

Ondo Finance filed a Form D (Regulation D exempt offering) with the SEC for OUSG (Ondo Short-Term US Government Bond Fund). This is a standard private placement exemption filing, not an enforcement action. Confirms OUSG is offered only to accredited investors.

ONDO token launched publicly. No SEC enforcement action or Wells Notice identified via EDGAR free search as of research date. Token is structured as a governance token; Ondo has been careful to avoid explicit yield promises on the token itself.
Ondo Finance announced expansion into international markets including Middle East. No regulatory violations cited. Ongoing monitoring of cross-border RWA tokenization regulatory landscape required.

founder_credibility_summary: Nathan Allman and Justin Schmidt bring rare Goldman Sachs digital assets pedigree to Ondo, lending strong institutional credibility that is verifiable via public LinkedIn profiles and press coverage. The team's TradFi-native background is directly aligned with Ondo's RWA tokenization thesis, reducing execution risk relative to crypto-native-only founding teams.
vc_overhang_summary: Ondo raised ~$24M across seed and Series A from top-tier VCs (Pantera, Founders Fund, Tiger Global, Coinbase Ventures); specific vesting schedules and unlock cliffs are NOT fully disclosed in public sources, creating meaningful VC overhang uncertainty. The ONDO tokenomics document indicates a large portion of supply is allocated to investors and team with multi-year vesting, meaning sustained sell pressure from insiders is a material risk as cliffs expire post-2025.
alignment_summary: Insider and retail incentives are PARTIALLY aligned but carry notable tension. Positives: the team has strong reputational skin-in-the-game, OUSG AUM growth directly benefits the protocol, and the governance token structure avoids explicit yield promises that could trigger SEC action. Risks: VC and team token allocations dwarf the public float at launch, vesting unlock schedules are not fully transparent in free-tier sources, and Wintermute's dual role as investor and market maker creates a potential conflict. Retail buyers should treat ONDO as a high-VC-overhang governance token until full vesting schedules are publicly confirmed.
